title: -Authorization to Apply for CalRecycle Waste Reduction, Reuse, Recycling, and Composting Grants and Payment Programs

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to submit grant applications, accept
grant funds, and execute any resulting grant agreements, amendments and expenditure
reports with the Department of Resource Recycling and Recovery (CalRecycle) for
grant and payment programs related to waste reduction, reuse, recycling, and
composting.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The City of Berkeley is eligible to apply for various CalRecycle grants in amounts
ranging from $10,000 to $500,000 annually. No matching funds are required. The grant
funds are utilized to implement programs related to achieving the City’s Zero Waste
goals through source-reduction, reuse, recycling, and/or composting.
sections.current_situation:
CalRecycle releases numerous grants and payment programs on an annual basis that
help fund City of Berkeley waste reduction and recycling programs. CalRecycle requires
an authorized Council Resolution to allow the City to apply for any CalRecycle
competitive and non-competitive grants and payment programs. CalRecycle has also
required that the City update its resolution for the SB 1383 Local Assistance Grant
Program (LAGP).
CalRecycle routinely provides funds to support implementation of City ordinances, such
as the Single Use Foodware and Litter Reduction Ordinance, and state legislative
requirements, such as SB1383 the Short-Lived Climate Pollutants Reduction Act. The
attached resolution meets CalRecycle’s grant application requirements that will allow
the City to apply for any available grants for which the City is eligible to apply within a
five-year time frame. Five years is the maximum length of time for an authorizing City
Council Resolution allowable by CalRecycle.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@CityofBerkeley.info Website: http://www.CityofBerkeley.info/Manager

Internal
Authorization to Apply for CalRecycle Waste Reduction, Reuse,
Recycling, and Compost Program Grants and Payment Programs July 29, 2025
sections.background:
The Public Works Department applies for and manages CalRecycle non-competitive
grants and payment programs, such as the Local Assistance Grant Program,
City/County Beverage Container Recycling Payment Program and the Used Oil
Payment Program.
The City has an existing Resolution authorizing the City Manager to apply for the
City/County Beverage Container Recycling Payment Program (Resolution No. 68,698-
N.S.), Used Oil Payment Program (Resolution No. 65,028-N.S.), and Local Assistance
Grant Program (Resolution; No. 69,546-N.S.); however, these resolutions are specific to
these payment programs and do not allow the City to apply for any other CalRecycle
competitive or non-competitive grants, which often have short application timelines. The
City has an existing Resolution authorizing the City Manager to apply for all CalRecycle
grants (Resolution No. 56,776-N.S.); however, that resolution was approved November
24, 1992.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
CalRecycle grant funding is utilized to implement waste-reduction, reuse, recycling and
composting programs that help achieve the City’s Zero Waste goal and Climate Action
Plan Goals.
sections.rationale:
If approved, the attached resolution will allow staff to apply for CalRecycle grants and
payment programs as they become available to help fund programs to reduce waste
and benefit community members.
sections.alternatives:
None. Without an updated resolution, staff cannot apply for CalRecycle grant funding, or
other available waste reduction, recycling, and composting grants.
CONTACT PERSON
Julia A. Heath, Recycling Program Manager, Public Works, (510) 981-6357.
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
Internal
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING SUBMITTAL OF APPLICATIONS FOR ALL
CALRECYCLE GRANTS FOR WHICH THE CITY OF BERKELEY IS ELIGIBLE
WHEREAS, Public Resources Code sections 48000 et seq. authorize the Department of
Resources Recycling and Recovery (CalRecycle) to administer various competitive, non-
competitive grant and payment program funds in furtherance of the State of California’s
(state) efforts to reduce, recycle and reuse solid waste generated in the state thereby
preserving landfill capacity and protecting public health and safety and the environment;
and
WHEREAS, in furtherance of this authority CalRecycle is required to establish procedures
governing the application, awarding, and management of the grants; and
WHEREAS, CalRecycle grant application procedures require, among other things, an
applicant’s governing body to declare by resolution certain authorizations related to the
administration of CalRecycle grants.
NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED that the City of Berkeley authorizes the submittal
of application(s) to CalRecycle for all grants for which the City of Berkeley is eligible.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that the City Manager, or his/her designee, is hereby
authorized and empowered to execute in the name of the City of Berkeley all grant
documents, including but not limited to, applications, agreements, amendments and
requests for payment, necessary to secure grant funds and implement the approved grant
project.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that these authorizations are effective for five (5) years
from the date of adoption of this resolution.
